title: 형이랑 같이 볼 영화

goal.ko: 동사 과거 관형(본 영화)과 동사 미래 관형(볼 영화)으로 명사를 꾸며 말할 수 있어요!
goal.en: I can describe a noun using verb past modifiers (the movie I watched) and verb future modifiers (the movie I'll watch)!

key_points: 동사 어간 + -(으)ㄴ + 명사 — 과거, 이미 한 일 (본 영화, 먹은 것)
동사 어간 + -(으)ㄹ + 명사 — 미래, 아직 안 한 일 (볼 영화, 마실 주스)
관형절 2단계 완성! (u09 형용사·동사현재 + u10 동사과거·미래 = 관형절 마스터)

sections.title: -(으)ㄴ + 명사
sections.subtitle: 동사 과거 관형 — Describing a noun with something already done
sections.description: To describe a noun with something that ALREADY happened — like 'the movie I watched' — attach -(으)ㄴ to the verb stem. It looks just like the 형용사 관형 from Episode 9, but on a 동사 it means PAST, not 'right now'!

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 받침 없으면 → -ㄴ
examples: 보다 → 본 영화 (the movie I watched)
가다 → 간 곳 (the place I went)

rule_boxes.label: ✅ 받침 있으면 → -은
examples: 먹다 → 먹은 것 (the thing I ate)
읽다 → 읽은 책 (the book I read)

rule_boxes.label: 💡 형용사 -(으)ㄴ vs 동사 -(으)ㄴ
examples: 본 영화 (동사 — 이미 한 일, 과거!)

rule_boxes.label: 🔴 조심! 있다로 끝나는 말은 항상 -는
examples: 있다/없다로 끝나면 형용사 같아도 동사처럼 -는을 써요

examples.korean: 어제 본 영화를 또 보는 거야?
examples.english: Are you watching the movie you watched yesterday, again?

examples.korean: 너무 재미있는 영화라서 또 보는 거예요.
examples.english: It's such a fun movie so I'm watching it again.

sections.title: -(으)ㄹ + 명사
sections.subtitle: 동사 미래 관형 — Describing a noun with something not done yet
sections.description: To describe a noun with something that HASN'T happened yet — like 'the juice I'll drink' — attach -(으)ㄹ to the verb stem.

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 받침 없으면 → -ㄹ
examples: 마시다 → 마실 주스 (the juice I'll drink)
보다 → 볼 영화 (the movie I'll watch)

rule_boxes.label: ✅ 받침 있으면 → -을
examples: 먹다 → 먹을 것 (the thing I'll eat)
읽다 → 읽을 책 (the book I'll read)

rule_boxes.label: 💡 Episode 7 콜백!
examples: 선물을 살 돈이 없어 — 그때 자연스럽게 썼던 문법이 이제 core로 돌아왔어요!

examples.korean: 카요, 영화를 보면서 마실 주스를 가지고 왔어.
examples.english: Kayo, I brought juice for you to drink while watching the movie.

examples.korean: 내일 형이랑 같이 볼 영화가 있어요.
examples.english: There's a movie I'm going to watch with my brother tomorrow.

sections.title: 본 vs 볼
sections.subtitle: 같은 동사, 다른 시제! Same verb, different tense
sections.description: The verb 보다 (to watch) turns into two completely different forms depending on tense — and they only differ by ONE final consonant! This episode is the perfect place to compare them side by side.

rule_boxes.label: ⚠️ 딱 받침 하나 차이! ㄴ vs ㄹ
examples: 헷갈리지 않게 천천히 연습해요 — 본(ㄴ) = 이미, 볼(ㄹ) = 아직

examples.korean: 어제 본 영화를 또 보는 거야?
examples.english: Are you watching the movie you watched yesterday, again?

examples.korean: 내일 형이랑 같이 볼 영화가 있어요.
examples.english: There's a movie I'm going to watch with my brother tomorrow.

lines.speaker: 할머니
lines.text: 카요, 영화를 보면서 마실 주스를 가지고 왔어.
lines.en: Kayo, I brought juice for you to drink while watching the movie.

lines.speaker: 할머니
lines.text: 어제 본 영화를 또 보는 거야?
lines.en: Are you watching the movie you watched yesterday, again?

lines.speaker: 카요
lines.text: 너무 재미있는 영화라서 또 보는 거예요.
lines.en: It's such a fun movie so I'm watching it again.

dialogue_practice.scene_en: The movie to watch with my brother

lines.speaker: 할머니
lines.en: Isn't there a new movie worth watching?

lines.speaker: 카요
lines.text: 내일 형이랑 같이 볼 영화가 있어요.
lines.en: There's a movie I'm going to watch with my brother tomorrow.

lines.speaker: 카요
lines.text: 할머니는 안 좋아하실 내용이에요. 무서운 영화거든요.
lines.en: It's the kind of story you wouldn't like, Grandma. It's a scary movie.

lines.speaker: 할머니
lines.en: A movie with robots in it?

lines.speaker: 카요
lines.en: No. It's a movie with a space goblin in it.

fill_items.en: Are you watching the movie you watched yesterday, again?
I brought juice for you to drink while watching the movie.
There's a movie I'm going to watch with my brother tomorrow.
It's such a fun movie so I'm watching it again.
A movie with robots in it?

listen_quiz.audio: 어제 본 영화를 또 보는 거야?

choices.en: Watching the movie from yesterday again

listen_quiz.audio: 내일 형이랑 같이 볼 영화가 있어요.

choices.en: Will watch a movie with brother tomorrow
Watched a movie with brother yesterday
Won't watch a movie with brother
It's a movie with a robot
It's a movie with a dog
It's a movie with a space goblin

step1_chunks.sentence: 영화를 보면서 마실 주스를 가지고 왔어.
step1_chunks.english: I brought juice for you to drink while watching the movie.

step1_chunks.sentence: 어제 본 영화를 또 보는 거야?
step1_chunks.english: Are you watching the movie you watched yesterday, again?
It's a movie with a space goblin in it.
step1_chunks.hint: 나오다 → 나오는 + 명사 (동사 현재 관형, u09 복습)

step2_words.english: I'm watching the movie I watched yesterday, again.

step2_words.sentence: 내일 형이랑 같이 볼 영화가 있어요.
step2_words.english: There's a movie I'm going to watch with my brother tomorrow.

write_items.question: 🎬 어제 이 영화를 봤어요. 오늘 또 보고 싶어요. 뭐라고 해요?
write_items.question_en: You watched this movie yesterday. You want to watch it again today. How do you say it?
write_items.answer_en: I'm watching the movie I watched yesterday, again.

write_items.question: 🥤 아직 안 마셨는데, 이따가 마실 거예요. 뭐라고 해요?
write_items.question_en: You haven't drunk it yet, but you will later. How do you say it?

dialogues.title: 📚 읽은 책, 읽을 책
dialogues.title_en: The book I read, the book I'll read

lines.en: Was the book you read last week fun?

lines.kr: 응! 그래서 다음에 읽을 책도 벌써 골랐어.
lines.en: Yeah! So I already picked the next book I'll read too.

dialogues.title: 🍕 먹은 것, 먹을 것
dialogues.title_en: What I ate, what I'll eat

lines.en: What did you eat for lunch?

lines.kr: 피자! 저녁에 먹을 것도 벌써 정했어.
lines.en: Pizza! I already decided what I'll eat for dinner too.

self_check.ko: 동사 어간 + -(으)ㄴ으로 이미 한 일을 말할 수 있어요. (본 영화)
self_check.en: I can use verb + -(으)ㄴ to describe something already done (the movie I watched).

self_check.ko: 동사 어간 + -(으)ㄹ로 아직 안 한 일을 말할 수 있어요. (볼 영화)
self_check.en: I can use verb + -(으)ㄹ to describe something not done yet (the movie I'll watch).
I know the difference between 본 (past) and 볼 (future).

self_check.ko: 영화, 내용, 도깨비, 우주, 나오다, 마시다를 알아요.
self_check.en: I know 영화, 내용, 도깨비, 우주, 나오다, and 마시다.
